title: RevolveFeature Object
description: Object that represents an existing revolve feature in a design.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createForAssemblyContext
methods.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

methods.name: dissolve
methods.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

methods.name: setAngleExtent
methods.description: Defines the extent of the revolution to be at a defined angle.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: setOneSideToExtent
methods.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be from the sketch plane to the specified "to" face.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: setTwoSideAngleExtent
methods.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be defined as a two sided angle extent.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

methods.name: setTwoSidesToExtent
methods.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be defined as a two sided to extent.   To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: assemblyContext
properties.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: attributes
properties.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

properties.name: axis
properties.description: Gets and sets the entity used to define the axis of revolution. The axis can be a sketch line, construction axis, linear edge or a face that defines an axis (cylinder, cone, torus, etc.). If it is not in the same plane as the profile, it is projected onto the profile plane.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: baseFeature
properties.description: If this feature is associated with a base feature, this property will return that base feature. If it's not associated with a base feature, this property will return null.

properties.name: bodies
properties.description: Returns the bodies that were modified or created by this feature. This property works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   For a BaseFeature, this property has two different behaviors depending on if the BaseFeature is active for edit or not. When the base feature is active, this property returns the bodies owned by the base feature or the source bodies. When the base feature is not active, this property returns the result bodies.   When a body is added to a base feature, that body is owned by the base feature and is only seen in the UI when the base feature is active. This body is referred to as a "source body". Fusion creates a parametric copy of the body when you exit the base feature. This copy is referred to as the "result body," and it is used for subsequent modeling operations.   You can map between the source and result bodies by using their position within the bodies returned. To get a valid list of result bodies, you should roll the timeline to immediately after the base feature node in the timeline. Otherwise, subsequent operations could have done something to cause one or more bodies to no longer be available.

properties.name: endFaces
properties.description: Property that returns the set of faces that cap the end of the revolve opposite the start faces. In the case where there aren't any start faces, this property will return null.

properties.name: entityToken
properties.description: Returns a token for the Feature object. This can be saved and used at a later time with the Design.findEntityByToken method to get back the same feature.   When using entity tokens it's important to understand that the token string returned for a specific entity can be different over time. However, even if you have two different token strings that were obtained from the same entity, when you use findEntityByToken they will both return the same entity. Because of that you should never compare entity tokens as way to determine what the token represents. Instead, you need to use the findEntityByToken method to get the two entities identified by the tokens and then compare them.

properties.name: errorOrWarningMessage
properties.description: Returns the error or warning message in the case where the healthState property returns either WarningFeatureHealthState or ErrorFeatureHealthState. Otherwise this property returns an empty string.

properties.name: extentDefinition
properties.description: Gets the definition object that is defining the extent of the revolve. Modifying the definition object will cause the revolve to recompute. Various types of objects can be returned depending on the type of extent currently defined for the revolve. This property returns nothing in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

properties.name: faces
properties.description: Returns the faces that were created by this feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.   Depending on the modifications made to the body after the feature was created, this may not return all of the faces. For example, when a component is created from a body, the original body and its faces no longer exist, so this won't return any faces. However, you can roll the timeline to immediately after the feature, and all the original faces will be available.

properties.name: healthState
properties.description: Returns the current health state of the feature.

properties.name: isParametric
properties.description: Indicates if this feature is parametric or not.

properties.name: isProjectAxis
properties.description: Specifies if the axis should be projected on the same plane as the profile sketch plane or not.   Setting this to true will use a projected axis, while setting it to false will keep it in its original location. This is initialized to false so the selected axis will be used in the feature.

properties.name: isSolid
properties.description: Indicates if this feature was initially created as a solid or a surface.

properties.name: isSuppressed
properties.description: Gets and sets if this feature is suppressed. This is only valid for parametric features.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: linkedFeatures
properties.description: Returns the set of features that are linked to this feature. The set of linked features are all of the features that were created in various components as the result of a single feature being created in the user interface.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Returns the name of the feature as seen in the browser (non-parametric) or in the timeline (parametric).

properties.name: nativeObject
properties.description: The NativeObject is the object outside the context of an assembly and in the context of its parent component. Returns null in the case where this object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: operation
properties.description: Gets and sets the type of operation performed by the revolve.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: parentComponent
properties.description: Returns the parent component that owns this feature.

properties.name: participantBodies
properties.description: Gets and sets the list of bodies that will participate in the feature when the operation is a cut or intersection.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

properties.name: profile
properties.description: Gets and sets the profiles or planar faces used to define the shape of the revolve. This property can return or be set with a single Profile, a single planar face, or an ObjectCollection consisting of multiple profiles and planar faces. When an ObjectCollection is used all of the profiles and faces must be co-planar.   When setting this property of a surface (non-solid) extrusion, you can use the createOpenProfile and createBRepEdgeProfile methods of the Component object to create an open profile.   To use this property, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)   This property returns null in the case where the feature is non-parametric.

properties.name: sideFaces
properties.description: Property that returns all of the faces created around the perimeter of the feature.

properties.name: startFaces
properties.description: Property that returns the set of faces that cap one end of the revolve and are coincident with the sketch plane. In the case of a symmetric revolve these faces are the ones on the positive normal side of the sketch plane. In the case where there aren't any start faces, this property will return null.

properties.name: timelineObject
properties.description: Returns the timeline object associated with this feature.

assemblyContext.title: RevolveFeature.assemblyContext Property
assemblyContext.description: Returns the assembly occurrence (i.e. the occurrence) of this object in an assembly. This is only valid in the case where this is acting as a proxy in an assembly. Returns null in the case where the object is not in the context of an assembly but is already the native object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = revolveFeature_var.assemblyContext

attributes.description: Returns the collection of attributes associated with this face.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = revolveFeature_var.attributes

classType.title: RevolveFeature.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.fusion.RevolveFeature.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::fusion::RevolveFeature::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createForAssemblyContext.title: RevolveFeature.createForAssemblyContext Method
createForAssemblyContext.description: Creates or returns a proxy for the native object - i.e. a new object that represents this object but adds the assembly context defined by the input occurrence.

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.createForAssemblyContext(occurrence)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->createForAssemblyContext(occurrence);

createForAssemblyContext.return_description: Returns the proxy object or null if this isn't the NativeObject.

parameters.name: occurrence
parameters.description: The occurrence that defines the context to create the proxy in.

deleteMe.title: RevolveFeature.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes the feature. This works for both parametric and non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.deleteMe()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->deleteMe();

deleteMe.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the delete was successful or not.

dissolve.title: RevolveFeature.dissolve Method
dissolve.description: Dissolves the feature so that the feature information is lost and only the B-Rep geometry defined by the feature remains. This is only valid for non-parametric features.

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.dissolve()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->dissolve();

dissolve.return_description: Returns a bool indicating if the dissolve was successful or not.

setAngleExtent.title: RevolveFeature.setAngleExtent Method
setAngleExtent.description: Defines the extent of the revolution to be at a defined angle. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.setAngleExtent(isSymmetric, angle)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->setAngleExtent(isSymmetric, angle);

parameters.name: isSymmetric
parameters.description: Boolean that specifies if the extent is symmetric or not.

parameters.name: angle
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the angle. This can be a string or a value. If it's a string it is interpreted using the current document units and can include equations. For example all of the following are valid as long as they result in angle units; "45", "45 deg", "a1 / 2". If a value is input it is interpreted as radians.   If isSymmetric is false a positive or negative angle can be used to control the direction. If isSymmetric is true, the angle is the extent in one direction so the entire angle of the revolution will be twice the specified angle. Use an angle of 360 deg or 2 pi radians to create a full revolve.

setOneSideToExtent.title: RevolveFeature.setOneSideToExtent Method
setOneSideToExtent.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be from the sketch plane to the specified "to" face. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = revolveFeature_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = revolveFeature_var.setOneSideToExtent(toEntity, directionHint)

parameters.name: toEntity
parameters.description: The entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.

parameters.name: directionHint
parameters.description: Specifies the direction of the revolve.   This is an optional argument whose default value is null.

setTwoSideAngleExtent.title: RevolveFeature.setTwoSideAngleExtent Method
setTwoSideAngleExtent.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be defined as a two sided angle extent. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.setTwoSideAngleExtent(angleOne, angleTwo)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->setTwoSideAngleExtent(angleOne, angleTwo);

parameters.name: angleOne
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the first angle. This can be a string or a value. If it's a string it is interpreted using the current document units and can include equations. For example all of the following are valid as long as they result in angle units; "45", "45 deg", "a1 / 2". If a value is input it is interpreted as radians.

parameters.name: angleTwo
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the second angle. This can be a string or a value. If it's a string it is interpreted using the current document units and can include equations. For example all of the following are valid as long as they result in angle units; "45", "45 deg", "a1 / 2". If a value is input it is interpreted as radians.

setTwoSidesToExtent.title: RevolveFeature.setTwoSidesToExtent Method
setTwoSidesToExtent.description: Changes the extent of the revolve to be defined as a two sided to extent. To use this method, you need to position the timeline marker to immediately before this feature. This can be accomplished using the following code: thisFeature.timelineObject.rollTo(True)

syntax.python: returnValue = revolveFeature_var.setTwoSidesToExtent(toEntityOne, toEntityTwo)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = revolveFeature_var->setTwoSidesToExtent(toEntityOne, toEntityTwo);

parameters.name: toEntityOne
parameters.description: The first entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.

parameters.name: toEntityTwo
parameters.description: The second entity that defines the "to" extent. The valid types of entities can vary depending on the type of feature this is being used with. For a revolve it can be a BRepBody, BRepFace, BRepVertex, ConstructionPlane, or ConstructionPoint.
